task_text:
Add elapsed time display to TAP reporter output

The TAP reporter in `reporters/catch_reporter_tap.cpp` outputs `ok`/`not ok` lines but does not include execution time for individual test cases. Add `# time=<ms>` directives after each test line, following the TAP 13 specification for timing metadata.
solve_notes: Three coordinated changes: (1) Added `#include <catch2/catch_timer.hpp>` and `Timer m_testCaseTimer` field to `catch_reporter_tap.hpp`. (2) Overrode `testCaseStarting` in the .cpp to call `m_testCaseTimer.start()` after delegating to base. (3) Overrode `testCaseEnded` to output `# time=<elapsed>s\n` before delegating to base. `TestCaseStats` does not carry a `durationInSeconds` field; timing information for test cases is only available via `SectionStats::durationInSeconds` from the top-level section, or via an explicit `Timer`. The `XmlReporter` uses the identical `Timer m_testCaseTimer` pattern, which was used as the model here. The TAP 13 spec allows `# time=Xs` comment directives after any test line.

search_sequence.result: `TAPReporter` is a `StreamingReporterBase` subclass; only overrides `testRunStarting`, `noMatchingTestCases`, `assertionEnded`, `testRunEnded`; no `testCaseStarting`/`testCaseEnded`; no timer field
search_sequence.reasoning: Need to see the existing class structure to know what to add

search_sequence.result: `TestCaseStats` has no `durationInSeconds`; `SectionStats` has `durationInSeconds`; timing is only available via SectionStats or an external timer
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to verify whether TestCaseStats could provide duration directly â€” it cannot

search_sequence.result: `XmlReporter` uses `Timer m_testCaseTimer`; calls `start()` in `testCaseStarting` and `getElapsedSeconds()` in `testCaseEnded` â€” exact pattern to replicate
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed a concrete example of per-test-case timing in a streaming reporter; XML reporter is the direct model

search_sequence.result: `Timer::start()` and `Timer::getElapsedSeconds() -> double` confirmed; no other includes needed in .cpp
search_sequence.reasoning: Need to know the exact method names to call

dead_ends.reason: Would require tracking which section corresponds to the top-level test case; much more complex than using an explicit Timer, and the XML reporter pattern is cleaner

key_decisions.decision: Output `# time=Xs\n` in `testCaseEnded` rather than after each `ok`/`not ok` assertion line
alternatives: Output timing after each individual assertion
key_decisions.reasoning: Per-test-case timing is the TAP 13 convention; outputting in testCaseEnded gives one timing line per test case (after all its assertions), which is the most useful granularity for TAP consumers

key_decisions.decision: Delegate to `StreamingReporterBase::testCaseEnded` after outputting the timing line
key_decisions.reasoning: Outputting the timing line before the base call ensures the line appears in the TAP stream immediately after the test case's assertion lines, before any state changes the base might perform

exploration_log.aha_moment: Finding that `XmlReporter` already uses the exact same `Timer m_testCaseTimer` pattern in `testCaseStarting`/`testCaseEnded` meant the entire implementation was a copy-adapt exercise.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have grepped for `testCaseTimer` across all reporters first, which would have immediately shown the XML pattern before reading `catch_interfaces_reporter.hpp` to check if `TestCaseStats` had a duration field.

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: TAPReporter::assertionEnded
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: read: shows where test lines are output and establishes the output pattern; the new testCaseEnded adds one line after all assertions for a test case are complete

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: TAPReporter
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: edited: added `#include <catch2/catch_timer.hpp>`, `testCaseStarting` and `testCaseEnded` overrides, and `Timer m_testCaseTimer` member

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: Timer
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: read: needed to confirm `start()` and `getElapsedSeconds()` API before writing the implementations

thrash_preventing_defs.name: XmlReporter::testCaseStarting
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: provides the exact code pattern â€” `m_testCaseTimer.start()` â€” preventing an agent from inventing a different timing approach

thrash_preventing_defs.name: XmlReporter::testCaseEnded
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: shows `m_testCaseTimer.getElapsedSeconds()` usage in testCaseEnded and the delegate pattern `StreamingReporterBase::testCaseEnded(testCaseStats)`, preventing the agent from forgetting to call the base

thrash_preventing_defs.name: TestCaseStats
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirmed no `durationInSeconds` field exists on TestCaseStats, ruling out a simpler approach and confirming the Timer pattern is necessary

tier_difference_reasoning: minimum_sufficient is assertionEnded (output model), TAPReporter class (edited), and Timer (API). thrash_preventing adds XmlReporter::testCaseStarting and testCaseEnded (direct code template) and TestCaseStats struct (to confirm the simpler approach is unavailable). Without the XML reporter pattern, an agent might try SectionStats or add std::chrono directly.

excluded_defs.name: TAPReporter::testRunStarting
excluded_defs.reason: outputs global run metadata (rng-seed, filters); not modified and not needed to understand the per-test-case timing change

queries.query_text: TAP reporter does not include timing information for individual test cases in the output stream so consumers cannot measure how long each test took
queries.justification: Domain terms 'TAP reporter', 'timing information', 'individual test cases', 'consumers'. No symbol names. Pre-implementation framing of the missing feature.
The literal TAP comment directive `# time=` is the exact string added by the fix. Pre-implementation: a developer looking to implement TAP timing would search for this standard directive.

queries.query_text: TAPReporter, testCaseEnded, testCaseStarting, Timer, m_testCaseTimer, getElapsedSeconds
queries.justification: Six exact symbol names from the diff and related utilities. Pre-implementation: a developer tracing the test case lifecycle in the tap reporter would identify these names.

queries.query_text: StreamingReporterBase subclasses that override testCaseStarting and testCaseEnded to measure elapsed time
queries.justification: Uses 'StreamingReporterBase subclasses' relationship and 'elapsed time' concept. Pre-implementation: a developer looking for the timer pattern would ask for reporter subclasses that already measure timing.
Three explicit file paths from the solution. Pre-implementation: a developer adding timing to the TAP reporter would navigate to all three files.

queries.query_text: Add Timer-based per-test-case timing to TAPReporter by overriding testCaseStarting and testCaseEnded to output TAP time directive
queries.justification: Mixes domain concept 'TAP time directive' with exact symbols. Pre-implementation framing after identifying the reporter and timer types.

queries.query_text: TAPReporter testCaseStarting testCaseEnded Timer in catch_reporter_tap.hpp catch_reporter_tap.cpp
queries.justification: Symbol names plus file paths. Pre-implementation: a developer who has opened the files would search for these types together.

queries.query_text: In catch_reporter_tap.hpp add Timer m_testCaseTimer field and testCaseStarting/testCaseEnded overrides; in catch_reporter_tap.cpp implement testCaseStarting to call m_testCaseTimer.start() and testCaseEnded to output '# time=Xs'
queries.justification: Includes both files, field names, methods, and the literal output string. Pre-implementation framing after full root-cause analysis.

test_selection.test_query: Find tests that exercise TAPReporter::assertionEnded or TAPReporter::testRunStarting in catch_reporter_tap.cpp

relevant_preexisting_tests.test_name: Reporter's write listings to provided stream
relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: Instantiates all registered reporters including TAPReporter and exercises listing/startup code paths; drives testRunStarting through the reporter factory
